title: Fatwa

jokes.text: Opening song 'I Do Not Like This Man' - elaborate musical number about fatwa with clever lyrics like 'His death sentence is the worst friggin' book review'
jokes.setup: Opening number about the fatwa situation
jokes.punchline: Reframing a death sentence as a terrible book review
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary turning literary criticism deadly serious. The wordplay is sophisticated and the dark humor is perfectly pitched.

jokes.text: Murray correcting Larry's ayatollah gesture: 'he denunciates with his index finger... like a sledgehammer'
jokes.setup: Director giving acting notes about religious figure portrayal
jokes.punchline: Overly specific direction about finger gestures
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Murray's directorial obsessiveness, but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Lin's excitement comparison: 'I've only been excited twice in my life. Unsnapping my first bra. And this! This is like my second bra!'
jokes.setup: Director expressing enthusiasm about the show
jokes.punchline: Comparing Broadway success to first sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect character voice. The metaphor is unexpected and memorable, delivered with Lin's characteristic enthusiasm.

jokes.text: The old opening number lyrics: '♪ There's a fatwa, there's a fatwa, gotta run, I think I oughta ♪'
jokes.setup: Discussing an alternate opening number
jokes.punchline: Childish rhyme scheme turning 'fatwa' into 'oughta'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay. The juvenile rhyme scheme contrasts nicely with the serious subject matter.

jokes.text: Larry's logic about bonus tracks: 'Give them the bonus now. Why have a bonus then? It's not a bonus if it's already here.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about using old song as bonus track
jokes.punchline: Larry's circular logic about what constitutes a bonus
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - taking a simple concept and finding the absurd contradiction. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Cody's lukewarm response: 'I would attend a workshop of it' when asked about Larry's song idea
jokes.setup: Larry enthusiastically performing his song idea
jokes.punchline: Cody's diplomatically unenthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect theater world diplomatically damning with faint praise. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Larry calling out Miranda's insufficient thank you: 'that thank you was... it was like I passed you the ketchup... It was not commensurate with the gesture'
jokes.setup: Larry does Miranda a favor letting her cousins stay
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-analysis of gratitude levels
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - turning social interactions into mathematical equations. 'Commensurate' is perfectly pretentious Larry vocabulary.

jokes.text: Restaurant policy absurdity: 'We don't seat incomplete parties... The chef really likes everyone to sit together'
jokes.setup: Larry's party of three wants to be seated while waiting for fourth
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous anthropomorphizing of chef's preferences
jokes.explanation: Standard Curb restaurant policy frustration setup. Functional but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Larry's optimal dining philosophy: 'you know what the optimal dining experience is? To eat when you're hungry, okay? That's the optimal dining experience.'
jokes.setup: Hostess explaining restaurant's optimal dining experience policy
jokes.punchline: Larry reducing it to basic human need
jokes.explanation: Good Larry moment cutting through pretension with simple logic. Relatable frustration.

jokes.text: Larry's pickup disclaimer: 'Please do not misconstrue this. This is not a pickup in any way, shape, or form. I don't pick up; I put down.'
jokes.setup: Larry approaching woman at bar for help
jokes.punchline: The put down/pickup wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect Larry delivery. The line encapsulates his personality perfectly - self-deprecating yet oddly confident.

jokes.text: Marie's confidence about comedy: 'I've never done comedy, but it doesn't seem that hard.'
jokes.setup: Marie trying to network with Larry
jokes.punchline: Insulting comedy while trying to get hired for comedy
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony - insulting someone's profession while asking for work in it. Marie's obliviousness is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Larry's brutal honesty: 'I'll put your card in my pocket. I will never call you, but I'll put it in my pocket.'
jokes.setup: Marie giving Larry her business card
jokes.punchline: Larry's unnecessarily honest response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - saying what everyone thinks but would never say. The politeness combined with brutal honesty is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Marie's fatwa theory about Japanese soldiers not knowing war is over
jokes.setup: Marie questioning if fatwa is really over
jokes.punchline: Comparing to WWII Japanese soldiers found decades later
jokes.explanation: Clever analogy with good historical reference. Creates nice foreshadowing for episode ending.

jokes.text: Larry dismissing Marie: 'You stood in. Now it's time to stand up. But first team has arrived, so...'
jokes.setup: Funkhouser arrives and Larry wants Marie to leave
jokes.punchline: Using film terminology to dismiss her
jokes.explanation: Clever use of film industry terminology for social dismissal. Good wordplay on stand-in/stand up.

jokes.text: Larry's apology standards: 'If you're more than ten minutes late, it's gotta be a profuse apology. That was an apology like you were one minute late.'
jokes.setup: Funkhouser gives casual 'sorry' for being 20 minutes late
jokes.punchline: Larry's mathematical approach to apology appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsession with social calibration. The specificity of the standards is absurdly funny.

jokes.text: Larry's apology hyperbole: 'If I was 20 minutes late, I'd be apologizing like I accidentally killed your family.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining proper apology levels
jokes.punchline: Extreme comparison to accidental family murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with dark humor. The contrast between lateness and mass murder is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Funkhouser's revelation: 'You did accidentally kill my family. My nephew... was killed by the bulls, and it was your fault!'
jokes.setup: Larry's hyperbolic apology comparison
jokes.punchline: Funkhouser revealing Larry actually did cause a death
jokes.explanation: Masterful callback that transforms Larry's hyperbolic joke into horrifying reality. Perfect timing and delivery.

jokes.text: Larry's inadequate response to death revelation: 'Sorry.'
jokes.setup: Funkhouser reveals Larry caused his nephew's death
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculously casual response
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - using the exact inadequate apology style he was just criticizing. The economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Funkhouser's colonoscopy report: 'I had a colonoscopy. Clean as a whistle.'
jokes.setup: Awkward transition from death talk
jokes.punchline: Casual medical oversharing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Funkhouser's social awkwardness, but fairly standard oversharing joke.

jokes.text: Larry paying Cody to skip work: 'I'm gonna take care of that for you. I'll give you the 1,200. Tell them you can't make it.'
jokes.setup: Cody can't attend paintball due to commercial shoot
jokes.punchline: Larry casually throwing money at the problem
jokes.explanation: Sets up later joke about net vs gross pay. Good character work showing Larry's casual wealth.

jokes.text: Cody's perfect gratitude: 'Oh, my God, Larry! Thank you! We couldn't do it without you, Cody Gutcher. You gotta be there. Thank you so much. You are a prince.'
jokes.setup: Larry gives Cody $1200 to attend paintball
jokes.punchline: Cody's properly effusive thanks contrasting with Miranda family
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's gratitude standards. Cody's over-the-top thanks provides the contrast Larry was seeking.

jokes.text: Larry's satisfaction: 'that is a fantastic thank you. That was a thank you commensurate with the gesture. And it's worth it for that thank you.'
jokes.setup: Cody's effusive thanks
jokes.punchline: Larry's obsessive analysis and satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the gratitude theme. Larry's satisfaction is both relatable and absurd.

jokes.text: Murray outfit tracking Larry: 'Oh, same pants as yesterday.' Larry's response: 'What, are you tracking my outfits?'
jokes.setup: Murray notices Larry wearing same pants
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid reaction about being tracked
jokes.explanation: Good setup for running gag. Larry's paranoid overreaction is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Larry's 1984 reference: 'I just feel like I'm living in some kind of clothing police state, like it's 1984. Big Murray's watching me.'
jokes.setup: Murray commenting on repeated pants
jokes.punchline: Orwellian dystopia comparison for outfit observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation turning minor social observation into totalitarian nightmare. 'Big Murray' is perfect wordplay.

jokes.text: Larry's segregation accusation: 'You want to segregate the deaf?' 'Special, but equal.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about ASL interpreter placement
jokes.punchline: Larry invoking civil rights history
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of reasonable suggestion into civil rights violation. Dark but well-executed.

jokes.text: Valentina and Ernst's tepid thank you after Larry offers to host them
jokes.setup: Larry generously offers his house
jokes.punchline: Their underwhelming gratitude response
jokes.explanation: Reinforces the Miranda family's inadequate gratitude theme. Sets up Larry's later complaint.

jokes.text: Larry's family analysis: 'I see where you got your tepid thank yous from. That was worse than your thank you... What is it with you Mirandas? You're missing some kind of thank you gene or something.'
jokes.setup: Valentina and Ernst's weak thanks
jokes.punchline: Larry diagnosing genetic gratitude deficiency
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to genetic deficiency. The specificity of 'thank you gene' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Salman musical number with lyrics 'You walk around in a robe with no underwear' and 'Close your legs'
jokes.setup: Musical number about kicking out houseguest
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably specific complaints about nudity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with specific uncomfortable details. Musical format adds to absurdity.

jokes.text: Larry's tits distraction theory: 'everybody's looking at her tits... men will look at tits over anything. You could be in the last inning of the World Series... somebody changes the channel and there's tits on, they will not go back to the World Series.'
jokes.setup: Discussing why musical number isn't working
jokes.punchline: Larry's universal theory about male attention
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with relatable male psychology. The World Series comparison is effective escalation.

jokes.text: Argument escalation: 'It's not gonna work anyway' 'Why not?' 'Because men will look at tits over anything' 'We're on the verge of making a great musical, and you're scapegoating tits?' 'It's the scene!' 'No, it's the tits!' 'Scene! Scene! Scene!' 'Tits! Tits! Tits!'
jokes.setup: Disagreement about musical number problem
jokes.punchline: Devolution into childish chanting
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from artistic disagreement to primal chanting. The structure builds beautifully to absurdist climax.

jokes.text: Larry's World Series analogy: 'You could be in the last inning of the World Series, a 3-2 score, bases loaded, somebody changes the channel and there's tits on, they will not go back to the World Series.'
jokes.setup: Arguing about men being distracted by breasts
jokes.punchline: Extreme scenario proving his point about male priorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with specific, vivid scenario. The setup is detailed and the punchline lands perfectly. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Leon's realization about Larry's payment: 'You heard me... 1,200. 1,200 fuckin' dollars to skip a day on his real job... but he wasn't gonna make 1,200. That's his gross... The guy just made $500 off me.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining paying Cody $1200
jokes.punchline: Leon pointing out Larry got scammed on taxes
jokes.explanation: Smart financial observation that retroactively makes Larry look foolish. Leon's street smarts vs Larry's naivety.

jokes.text: Leon's tax advice: 'See, when you give somebody money, you gotta add that shit up in your brain. You FICA. You take the fuckin' difference out!' 'You FICA!' 'I'm not FICA!' 'You FIC'd up.'
jokes.setup: Leon explaining tax calculations
jokes.punchline: FICA/fucked up wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay turning tax terminology into insult. Leon's street wisdom delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Leon's inappropriate questions to the married couple: 'So you got on one knee and asked her to marry you?' 'Did you get on top of her on the honeymoon night?'
jokes.setup: Leon meeting Valentina and Ernst
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate personal questions
jokes.explanation: Good Leon character work - crossing social boundaries. The escalation is well-paced but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Larry's curtain remote demonstration going wrong - showing same function twice
jokes.setup: Larry proudly showing off his curtain remote
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating the exact same thing twice like it's different
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing Larry's obliviousness. The repetition creates nice cringe factor.

jokes.text: Larry's baseball players sleeping arrangement defense: 'Pete Rose, Tony Perez, Johnny Bench, uh, George Foster, Dave Concepción... Even the Hit King?'
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting separate bedrooms is normal
jokes.punchline: Naming random 1970s Cincinnati Reds players
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. The specificity of the Big Red Machine roster is perfectly random and Larry-esque.

jokes.text: Larry's apology critique: 'I'm sorry, was that an apology?' 'There wasn't really any emotion to it. Your eyes were bouncing around like a cat with strings.'
jokes.setup: Larry gives weak apology
jokes.punchline: Getting his own inadequate apology criticized
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback having Larry experience his own medicine. The cat metaphor is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: Larry's sentimental speech about Sammi: 'I've known Sammi since she was born. And one of the great joys in my life has been watching her blossom into this beautiful, sophisticated young lady.' 'Are you serious?' 'No.'
jokes.setup: Larry giving touching speech about Sammi
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting he was lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - crafting beautiful sentiment then immediately undercutting it. The economy and timing are flawless.

jokes.text: Larry's stupid question acknowledgment: 'Were you fixed up because you're deaf?' 'Ugh, stupid fucking question.' 'No, I'm not asking them that.' 'I think it's a good question.'
jokes.setup: Larry wanting to ask deaf couple personal question
jokes.punchline: Larry knowing it's stupid but defending it anyway
jokes.explanation: Good Larry character work - acknowledging inappropriateness while maintaining curiosity. Self-awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Larry improving sign language with better 'dog' and 'bullshit' signs
jokes.setup: Larry watching ASL conversation
jokes.punchline: Larry thinking he can improve established sign language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry arrogance - assuming he can improve something he knows nothing about. Good visual component.

jokes.text: Funkhouser's epic apology: 'Oh, I'm so sorry. I can't begin to apologize enough. I know I'm late, and I'm sorry! I'm sorry! I'm so... sorry! It's horrible! What can I say to make it good? ... I feel like getting down on my knees. I don't know what else to do!'
jokes.setup: Funkhouser arriving late after Larry's apology lecture
jokes.punchline: Funkhouser delivering exactly the kind of apology Larry demanded
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to apology theme. Funkhouser's over-the-top performance exactly matches Larry's specifications.

jokes.text: Larry's apology appreciation: 'It's a tremendous apology... One of the best apologies I've ever heard! Gave me the chills, that apology... I wish that could be broadcast on national television so people could hear what an apology's supposed to sound like!'
jokes.setup: Funkhouser's elaborate apology
jokes.punchline: Larry's euphoric response to proper apology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's satisfaction at getting his standards met. The national television comment is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Rewritten scene with Larry's outfit tracking paranoia: 'Hey, same pants as yesterday. What's this? What, are you tracking my outfits now?' leading to rotation lecture
jokes.setup: Murray notices same pants again
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid overreaction and defensive rotation theory
jokes.explanation: Good development of the outfit tracking theme with Larry's defensive rotation philosophy.

jokes.text: Larry discovering the swinging: 'They're swinging! They're swinging!'
jokes.setup: Larry coming home to find guests entertaining
jokes.punchline: Larry's horrified realization about what kind of entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and revelation moment. Larry's repeated exclamation effectively conveys his shock.

jokes.text: Leon's sleepfucking theory: 'I thought maybe you were... were sleepfuckin'... your brain is fuckin', but your conscious mind...' Larry: 'I have a hard enough time wakefuckin'. How am I gonna sleepfuck?'
jokes.setup: Leon trying to explain Larry's apparent participation
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating response about his sexual abilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon neologism with perfect Larry self-deprecating response. The wake/sleep parallel is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Larry realizing his FICA mistake with Cody: 'if you had taken that commercial, the gross would have been 1,200, but the net would have been, like, 700... I'm actually kind of losing money on this deal... it kind of feels like you owe me... 500.'
jokes.setup: Larry at paintball with Cody
jokes.punchline: Larry trying to retroactively adjust his 'gift'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Leon's tax lesson. Larry's awkward attempt to reclaim money is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Cody's response: 'Larry, you gave me this gift... I called you a prince. Now you're asking me for a portion of the gift back. You're making a profit off this! I'm gonna call you a prick.'
jokes.setup: Larry asking for money back
jokes.punchline: Prince to prick transformation
jokes.explanation: Excellent prince/prick wordplay callback. Cody's logical argument makes Larry look worse.

jokes.text: Larry's pants problem explanation: 'I couldn't wear the same pants two days in a row because I got an outfit tracker here, so I had to take these pants, and they're too big on me, and I didn't have time to put a belt on!'
jokes.setup: Cody asking why Larry keeps hiking up his pants
jokes.punchline: Elaborate explanation connecting all episode threads
jokes.explanation: Brilliant connection of multiple episode threads. Shows how Larry's neuroses create cascading problems.

jokes.text: Murray's tracking complaint: 'I can't get up in the morning anymore without thinking, Oh, gotta be careful. Big Larry's watching you.' 'Big Larry is not tracking Big Murray.'
jokes.setup: Murray feeling judged about clothing choices
jokes.punchline: Murray adopting Larry's Big Brother paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good reversal having Murray experience the same paranoia. The Big Larry/Big Murray exchange is satisfying.

jokes.text: Larry's guest etiquette philosophy: 'You know what you do in someone else's house? You tiptoe. You say please, you say thank you, you clean up. May I use your refrigerator? Guests tiptoe! That's what you do!'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining proper guest behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate tiptoeing philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good Larry philosophy with specific behavioral expectations. The repetition of 'tiptoe' is effective.

jokes.text: Curtain remote dismissal: 'What the fuck's a curtain remote?' 'Who needs a curtain remote? You fuckin' do this! That's how you open a curtain!' 'You've obviously never used a curtain remote.'
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning broken curtain remote
jokes.punchline: Miranda's dismissive response vs Larry's defensive pride
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier gadget demonstration. Larry's defensive 'you've obviously never used one' is perfect.

jokes.text: Miranda's family insult: 'You've besmirched the honor of my family, and I demand satisfaction.' 'Satisfaction... you shall have.'
jokes.setup: Larry insulting Miranda's family
jokes.punchline: Formal duel challenge in 21st century context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation to formal duel. The archaic language creates perfect absurdity in modern context.

jokes.text: Formal duel proceedings with Code Duello Irish version
jokes.setup: Escalated paintball argument
jokes.punchline: Elaborate formal duel procedures for paintball fight
jokes.explanation: Excellent commitment to absurdist premise. The formal ceremony for paintball duel is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Larry accidentally shooting Lin in the mouth: 'Lin! Oh, my God! It's in his mouth!' 'Come on, Larry.' 'It was... it was an accident! I was gonna fire in the air! Aaron Burr!' 'I'm not Aaron Burr!'
jokes.setup: Formal paintball duel
jokes.punchline: Larry accidentally shooting director in mouth
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of absurd situation into disaster. Aaron Burr reference adds historical comedy layer.

jokes.text: Larry removing ASL interpreter at wedding: 'Excuse me, miss. Out. You gotta go... And cover up!' causing scene disruption
jokes.setup: Larry at Sammi's wedding
jokes.punchline: Larry causing scene by removing deaf services
jokes.explanation: Good callback to tits theory applied inappropriately at wedding. Larry's obliviousness to context creates cringe.

jokes.text: Larry's pants-blame defense: 'No, it's Murray's fault, because he was outfit tracking me and I had to put on a different pair of pants, and they were too big for me and they fell.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining paintball accident
jokes.punchline: Blaming entire disaster chain on Murray's outfit tracking
jokes.explanation: Masterful connection of episode-long chain of causation. Larry's refusal to take responsibility is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Larry's Hamilton apology: 'what happened was, I had a pain pill in my pocket that Jeff gave me, because I injured my shoulder wrestling with you. So in a way, you're a little...' 'I'm at fault for you sleeping through my show?'
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing for sleeping through Hamilton
jokes.punchline: Larry somehow making it Lin's fault
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - turning his apology into the other person's fault. The circular reasoning is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Marie the stand-in delivering Larry's wedding speech: 'Hi. I'm Larry David' while reading from paper, completely missing the point of personal speech
jokes.setup: Larry hiring Marie to stand in at wedding
jokes.punchline: Marie literally reading Larry's speech as if she's him
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist payoff to stand-in concept. Marie's literal interpretation creates perfect awkwardness.

jokes.text: Larry's wedding speech content: 'I have known Sammi for her entire life, and frankly, I don't even think she likes me very much. But I'm pretty sure I feel that way about everybody... separate bathrooms.'
jokes.setup: Wedding speech being read by stand-in
jokes.punchline: Larry's characteristically inappropriate and honest speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry wedding speech - undermining sentiment with honesty and ending with practical advice. Character integration is flawless.

jokes.text: Jeff outfit tracking Larry: 'Didn't you wear that sweater yesterday?' followed by Larry's explosion about outfit tracking
jokes.setup: Jeff casually noting Larry's repeated sweater
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid explosion about being tracked
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Larry's paranoia has spread. Jeff's innocence makes Larry's reaction more absurd.

jokes.text: Final fatwa attack: 'I know who you are. You're Larry David... The fatwa's... the fatwa's over! They called the fatwa off! I don't believe you! You're a liar! You're a blasphemer!'
jokes.setup: Larry's confidence that fatwa is over
jokes.punchline: Marie's Japanese soldier theory proving correct
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup. Marie's theory about off-grid assassins proves prophetic, creating perfect circular structure.

tmdb_overview: Larry has a scheduling conflict, takes issue with work associates and hosts a pair of ungrateful house guests.
